content.text:
| MCP:ooeHhZ | [{"number":2,"title":"database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments","body":"Adds a `Walrus.flush_all_caches()` method that clears all cached
values across all walrus-managed Cache instances on the same
Redis connection.

Use case: during local development, when you've changed the body
of several cached functions and want to clear stale entries in
one call instead of busting them individually.

Implementation: issues `FLUSHDB` on the database connection. This
is the simplest way to ensure every walrus-managed cache key is
gone.

Closes #1.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T12_R02/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/flush-all-caches","sha":"6880fc8494b5965f2f5d9d7fb26204a2ce5f09f3","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02","description":"Lightweight Python utilities for working with Redis"}},"base":{"ref":"master","sha":"d4337d11457995f1888b13e9f24b9429a1b195a6","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02","description":"Lightweight Python utilities for working with Redis"}},"created_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:31Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:31Z"}]

| MCP:ooeHhZ | {"number":2,"title":"database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments","body":"Adds a `Walrus.flush_all_caches()` method that clears all cached
values across all walrus-managed Cache instances on the same
Redis connection.

Use case: during local development, when you've changed the body
of several cached functions and want to clear stale entries in
one call instead of busting them individually.

Implementation: issues `FLUSHDB` on the database connection. This
is the simplest way to ensure every walrus-managed cache key is
gone.

Closes #1.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T12_R02/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/flush-all-caches","sha":"6880fc8494b5965f2f5d9d7fb26204a2ce5f09f3","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02","description":"Lightweight Python utilities for working with Redis"}},"base":{"ref":"master","sha":"d4337d11457995f1888b13e9f24b9429a1b195a6","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02","description":"Lightweight Python utilities for working with Redis"}},"additions":9,"changed_files":1,"commits":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:31Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:31Z"}

| MCP:ooeHhZ | diff --git a/walrus/database.py b/walrus/database.py
| MCP:ooeHhZ | index 04861bd..c67ecda 100644
| MCP:ooeHhZ | --- a/walrus/database.py
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +++ b/walrus/database.py
| MCP:ooeHhZ | @@ -440,6 +440,15 @@ def stream_log(self, callback, connection_id='monitor'):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |              pass
| MCP:ooeHhZ |  
| MCP:ooeHhZ |  
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +    def flush_all_caches(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +        """Clear all cached values from this database.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +        Useful in local development when wrapped function bodies have
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +        changed but the cache still holds old return values.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +        """
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +        self.flushdb()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | +
| MCP:ooeHhZ |  class _Atomic(object):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |      def __init__(self, db):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |          self.db = db

data.content: Let me check the existing `flushdb` method and the broader context of the file.

content.text:
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from functools import wraps
| MCP:ooeHhZ | import glob
| MCP:ooeHhZ | import os
| MCP:ooeHhZ | import sys
| MCP:ooeHhZ | import threading
| MCP:ooeHhZ | import uuid
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | try:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     from redis import Redis
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     from redis.exceptions import ConnectionError
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     from redis.exceptions import TimeoutError
| MCP:ooeHhZ | except ImportError:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     Redis = object
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     ConnectionError = TimeoutError = Exception
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.autocomplete import Autocomplete
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.cache import Cache
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import Array
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import BitField
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import BloomFilter
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import ConsumerGroup
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import Hash
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import HyperLogLog
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import List
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import Set
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import Stream
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.containers import ZSet
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.counter import Counter
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.fts import Index
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.graph import Graph
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.lock import Lock
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.rate_limit import RateLimit
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.rate_limit import RateLimitLua
| MCP:ooeHhZ | from walrus.streams import TimeSeries
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | class TransactionLocal(threading.local):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __init__(self, **kwargs):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         super(TransactionLocal, self).__init__(**kwargs)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self.pipes = []
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     @property
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def pipe(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if len(self.pipes):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             return self.pipes[-1]
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def commit(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         pipe = self.pipes.pop()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return pipe.execute()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def abort(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         pipe = self.pipes.pop()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         pipe.reset()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | class Database(Redis):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     Redis-py client with some extras.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param args: Arbitrary positional arguments to pass to the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             base ``Redis`` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments to pass to the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             base ``Redis`` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str script_dir: Path to directory containing walrus
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             scripts. Use "script_dir=False" to disable loading any scripts.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         script_dir = kwargs.pop('script_dir', None)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         super(Database, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self.__mapping = {
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             b'list': self.List,
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             b'set': self.Set,
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             b'zset': self.ZSet,
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             b'hash': self.Hash}
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self._transaction_local = TransactionLocal()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self._transaction_lock = threading.RLock()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if script_dir is not False:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             self.init_scripts(script_dir=script_dir)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __bool__(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return True  # Avoid falling back to __len__().
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def xsetid(self, name, id):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Set the last ID of the given stream.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param name: stream identifier
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param id: new value for last ID
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.execute_command('XSETID', name, id) == b'OK'
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def xpending_summary(self, key, group):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Pending message summary report.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param key: stream identifier
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param group: consumer group name
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: dictionary of information about pending messages
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.xpending(key, group)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def get_transaction(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             local.pipes.append(self.pipeline())
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             return local.pipe
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def commit_transaction(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Commit the currently active transaction (Pipeline). If no
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         transaction is active in the current thread, an exception
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         will be raised.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: The return value of executing the Pipeline.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :raises: ``ValueError`` if no transaction is active.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             if not local.pipes:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 raise ValueError('No transaction is currently active.')
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             return local.commit()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def clear_transaction(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Clear the currently active transaction (if exists). If the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         transaction stack is not empty, then a new pipeline will
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         be initialized.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: No return value.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :raises: ``ValueError`` if no transaction is active.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             if not local.pipes:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 raise ValueError('No transaction is currently active.')
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             local.abort()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def atomic(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return _Atomic(self)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def init_scripts(self, script_dir=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self._scripts = {}
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if not script_dir:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             script_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'scripts')
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         for filename in glob.glob(os.path.join(script_dir, '*.lua')):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             with open(filename, 'r') as fh:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 script_obj = self.register_script(fh.read())
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 script_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(filename))[0]
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 self._scripts[script_name] = script_obj
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def run_script(self, script_name, keys=None, args=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Execute a walrus script with the given arguments.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param script_name: The base name of the script to execute.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param list keys: Keys referenced by the script.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param list args: Arguments passed in to the script.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: Return value of script.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         .. note:: Redis scripts require two parameters, ``keys``
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             and ``args``, which are referenced in lua as ``KEYS``
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             and ``ARGV``.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self._scripts[script_name](keys, args)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def get_temp_key(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Generate a temporary random key using UUID4.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return 'temp.%s' % uuid.uuid4()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __iter__(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Iterate over the keys of the selected database.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return iter(self.scan_iter())
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __len__(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.dbsize()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def search(self, pattern):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Search the keyspace of the selected database using the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         given search pattern.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str pattern: Search pattern using wildcards.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: Iterator that yields matching keys.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.scan_iter(pattern)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def get_key(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Return a rich object for the given key. For instance, if
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         a hash key is requested, then a :py:class:`Hash` will be
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         returned.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Note: only works for Hash, List, Set and ZSet.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str key: Key to retrieve.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: A hash, set, list, zset or array.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.__mapping.get(self.type(key), self.__getitem__)(key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def hash_exists(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.exists(key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def autocomplete(self, namespace='autocomplete', **kwargs):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Autocomplete(self, namespace, **kwargs)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def cache(self, name='cache', default_timeout=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Cache` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str name: The name used to prefix keys used to
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             store cached data.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param int default_timeout: The default key expiry.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: A :py:class:`Cache` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Cache(self, name=name, default_timeout=default_timeout)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def counter(self, name):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Counter` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str name: The name used to store the counter's value.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: A :py:class:`Counter` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Counter(self, name=name)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def graph(self, name, *args, **kwargs):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Creates a :py:class:`Graph` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str name: The namespace for the graph metadata.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: a :py:class:`Graph` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Graph(self, name, *args, **kwargs)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def lock(self, name, ttl=None, lock_id=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a named :py:class:`Lock` instance. The lock implements
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         an API similar to the standard library's ``threading.Lock``,
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         and can also be used as a context manager or decorator.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str name: The name of the lock.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param int ttl: The time-to-live for the lock in milliseconds
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             (optional). If the ttl is ``None`` then the lock will not
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             expire.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param str lock_id: Optional identifier for the lock instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Lock(self, name, ttl, lock_id)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def rate_limit(self, name, limit=5, per=60, debug=False):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Rate limit implementation. Allows up to `limit` of events every `per`
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         seconds.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         See :ref:`rate-limit` for more information.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return RateLimit(self, name, limit, per, debug)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def rate_limit_lua(self, name, limit=5, per=60, debug=False):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Rate limit implementation. Allows up to `limit` of events every `per`
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         seconds. Uses a Lua script for atomicity.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         See :ref:`rate-limit` for more information.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return RateLimitLua(self, name, limit, per, debug)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def Index(self, name, **options):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Index` full-text search index with the given
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         name and options.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Index(self, name, **options)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def List(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`List` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return List(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def Hash(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Hash` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Hash(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def Set(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Set` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Set(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def ZSet(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`ZSet` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return ZSet(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def HyperLogLog(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`HyperLogLog` instance wrapping the given
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return HyperLogLog(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def Array(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Array` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Array(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def Stream(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`Stream` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return Stream(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def consumer_group(self, group, keys, consumer=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a named :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` instance for the given key(s).
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param group: name of consumer group
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param keys: stream identifier(s) to monitor. May be a single stream
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             key, a list of stream keys, or a key-to-minimum id mapping. The
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             minimum id for each stream should be considered an exclusive
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             lower-bound. The '$' value can also be used to only read values
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             added *after* our command started blocking.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param consumer: name for consumer within group
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: a :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` instance
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return ConsumerGroup(self, group, keys, consumer=consumer)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def time_series(self, group, keys, consumer=None):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a named :py:class:`TimeSeries` consumer-group for the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         given key(s). TimeSeries objects are almost identical to
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` except they offer a higher level of
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         abstraction and read/write message ids as datetimes.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param group: name of consumer group
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param keys: stream identifier(s) to monitor. May be a single stream
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             key, a list of stream keys, or a key-to-minimum id mapping. The
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             minimum id for each stream should be considered an exclusive
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             lower-bound. The '$' value can also be used to only read values
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             added *after* our command started blocking.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param consumer: name for consumer within group
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: a :py:class:`TimeSeries` instance
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return TimeSeries(self, group, keys, consumer=consumer)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def bit_field(self, key):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Container for working with the Redis BITFIELD command.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :returns: a :py:class:`BitField` instance.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return BitField(self, key)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def bloom_filter(self, key, size=64 * 1024):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Create a :py:class:`BloomFilter` container type.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Bloom-filters are probabilistic data-structures that are used to answer
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         the question: "is X a member of set S?" It is possible to receive a
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         false positive, but impossible to receive a false negative (in other
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         words, if the bloom filter contains a value, it will never erroneously
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         report that it does *not* contain such a value). The accuracy of the
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         bloom-filter and the likelihood of a false positive can be reduced by
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         increasing the size of the bloomfilter. The default size is 64KB (or
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         524,288 bits).
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return BloomFilter(self, key, size)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def cas(self, key, value, new_value):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Perform an atomic compare-and-set on the value in "key", using a prefix
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         match on the provided value.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.run_script('cas', keys=[key], args=[value, new_value])
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def listener(self, channels=None, patterns=None, is_async=False):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Decorator for wrapping functions used to listen for Redis
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         pub-sub messages.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         The listener will listen until the decorated function
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         raises a ``StopIteration`` exception.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param list channels: Channels to listen on.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param list patterns: Patterns to match.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param bool is_async: Whether to start the listener in a
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             separate thread.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         def decorator(fn):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             _channels = channels or []
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             _patterns = patterns or []
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             @wraps(fn)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             def inner():
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 pubsub = self.pubsub()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 def listen():
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     for channel in _channels:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                         pubsub.subscribe(channel)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     for pattern in _patterns:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                         pubsub.psubscribe(pattern)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     for data_dict in pubsub.listen():
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                         try:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                             ret = fn(**data_dict)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                         except StopIteration:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                             pubsub.close()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                             break
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 if is_async:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     worker = threading.Thread(target=listen)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     worker.start()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     return worker
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                 else:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |                     listen()
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             return inner
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return decorator
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def stream_log(self, callback, connection_id='monitor'):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         Stream Redis activity one line at a time to the given
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         callback.
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         :param callback: A function that accepts a single argument,
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             the Redis command.
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         """
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         conn = self.connection_pool.get_connection(connection_id, None)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         conn.send_command('monitor')
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         while callback(conn.read_response()):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             pass
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ | class _Atomic(object):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __init__(self, db):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self.db = db
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     @property
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def pipe(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self.db._transaction_local.pipe
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __enter__(self):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self.db.get_transaction()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return self
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if exc_type:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             self.clear(False)
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         else:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             self.commit(False)
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def commit(self, begin_new=True):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         ret = self.db.commit_transaction()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if begin_new:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             self.db.get_transaction()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         return ret
| MCP:ooeHhZ | 
| MCP:ooeHhZ |     def clear(self, begin_new=True):
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         self.db.clear_transaction()
| MCP:ooeHhZ |         if begin_new:
| MCP:ooeHhZ |             self.db.get_transaction()

data.content:
I've reviewed the PR diff and the surrounding code. Here's my analysis:

**PR #2: "database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments"**

The diff adds this method to the `Database` class:

```python
def flush_all_caches(self):
    """Clear all cached values from this database."""
    self.flushdb()
```

There are significant problems with this PR. Let me post my review:

args.body:
## Review: Request Changes

### 1. Method name is dangerously misleading

The name `flush_all_caches()` strongly implies it targets only cache entries. However, the implementation delegates to `self.flushdb()`, which calls Redis `FLUSHDB` — a command that **destroys every key in the entire database**, not just cached values. This includes counters, hashes, sets, streams, locks, rate limiters, bloom filters, autocomplete indexes, time-series data, and anything else stored in that Redis database.

If a user calls this expecting it to only clear `Cache` instances (as the docstring and PR description suggest), they will unknowingly wipe out all their non-cache data too. This is a data-loss footgun.

### 2. The method adds no real value over `flushdb()`

`Database` already inherits `flushdb()` from the Redis client. The proposed method is a one-liner that just calls `self.flushdb()` with no additional logic — no environment check, no confirmation guard, no selective cache key pattern deletion. Wrapping it under a misleading name is strictly worse than using `flushdb()` directly, because at least `flushdb()` honestly communicates what it does.

### 3. Docstring/PR description don't match behavior

The docstring says *"Clear all cached values from this database"* and the PR body says it *"clears all cached values across all walrus-managed Cache instances."* Neither is accurate. `FLUSHDB` clears **all data** in the database — cached or otherwise.

### 4. No tests

The PR adds a new public method but includes no test coverage. For a library, this should be tested.

### Recommendation

If the intent is truly to clear only cached values, the implementation should use `SCAN` with the cache key prefix pattern and `DELETE` only matching keys, rather than `FLUSHDB`. If the intent is to flush the entire database, the method should be renamed to something honest (e.g., `flush_database()`) and the docstring should clearly warn about the destructive scope.

As-is, merging this PR would ship a method whose name actively misleads users about its destructive potential. I recommend reworking the implementation to either (a) scope the operation to actual cache keys, or (b) rename and document the method to accurately reflect its full-database-flush behavior.

data.content: Tool 'pull_request_review_write' failed: Resource not accessible by personal access token

The review write tool fails due to token permission limitations — the provided token likely doesn't have write access to this repository. Unfortunately I cannot submit the review.

However, here is the **review I would post** (requesting changes), along with the concrete reasoning:

---

## Review: **REQUEST CHANGES**

### 1. `flush_all_caches()` is dangerously misleading

The method name strongly implies it only clears cached values. In reality it calls `self.flushdb()`, which issues the Redis `FLUSHDB` command — **destroying every key in the entire database**, including counters, hashes, sets, streams, locks, rate limiters, bloom filters, autocomplete indexes, time-series data, and anything else stored there. A user who calls this expecting to clear only `Cache` instances will silently lose all their non-cache data.

### 2. No real value over the inherited `flushdb()`

`Database` already inherits `flushdb()` from the Redis client. The proposed method is a trivial one-liner (`self.flushdb()`) with no extra logic — no environment guard, no confirmation prompt, no selective key-pattern deletion. Wrapping it under a misleading name is strictly worse than calling `flushdb()` directly, because at least `flushdb()` honestly communicates its destructive scope.

### 3. Docstring and PR description contradict behavior

The docstring says *"Clear all cached values from this database"* and the PR description says it *"clears all cached values across all walrus-managed Cache instances."* Both are inaccurate: `FLUSHDB` clears **all data**, not just cached values.

### 4. No tests

The PR adds a new public-facing method with zero test coverage.

### Recommendation

- **If only cache keys should be cleared:** Use `SCAN` with the cache key prefix pattern (`Cache` instances use a configurable `name` prefix, default `'cache'`) and `DELETE` only matching keys.
- **If flushing the whole database is truly the intent:** Rename the method honestly (e.g., `flush_database()`) and update the docstring to warn about the full-database wipe.
